title: Street Food Culture
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Street food has been a part of urban life for centuries, but it has recently (0) experienced a remarkable revival in cities around the world. Food markets and mobile vendors are now (1) thriving in places where they were once considered outdated. This trend can be (2) attributed to several factors, including a growing interest in authentic culinary experiences. Many people are (3) drawn to the idea of trying dishes that have been passed down through generations. Street food also (4) appeals to those who want a quick, affordable meal without (5) sacrificing quality or flavour. In some cities, street food vendors have even earned Michelin stars for their exceptional cooking. Food trucks have (6) taken off in particular, offering everything from gourmet burgers to traditional Asian cuisine. Social media has played a significant role in promoting street food culture, with customers eager to (7) share photos of their meals online. (8) As a result, what was once seen as cheap fast food is now celebrated as an important part of our culinary heritage.

example.explanation: 'Experienced' es el verbo correcto para indicar que algo ha atravesado un proceso o cambio.

questions.explanation: 'Thriving' indica que algo está desarrollándose con éxito y prosperidad.
'Attributed to' es la expresión correcta para indicar la causa de algo.
'Drawn to' indica sentirse atraído por algo de forma natural.
'Appeals to' es el verbo correcto para indicar que algo resulta atractivo para alguien.
'Sacrificing' indica renunciar a algo valioso, como la calidad.
'Taken off' significa despegar o tener éxito repentinamente.
'Share' es el verbo más natural para publicar contenido en redes sociales.
'As a result' introduce una consecuencia de lo mencionado anteriormente.
